title: Polynomial structures for nilpotent groups
author_string: Dekimpe, Karel, Igodt, Paul, Lee, Kyung
journal: Transactions of the American Mathematical Society
abstract:
If a polycyclic-by-finite rank-

  
    K
    K
  

 group 

  
    Γ
    \Gamma
  

 admits a faithful affine representation making it acting on 

  
    
      
        R
      
      
        K
      
    
    \mathbb {R}{K}
  

 properly discontinuously and with compact quotient, we say that 

  
    Γ
    \Gamma
  

 admits an affine structure. In 1977, John Milnor questioned the existence of affine structures for such groups 

  
    Γ
    \Gamma
  

. Very recently examples have been obtained showing that, even for torsion-free, finitely generated nilpotent groups 

  
    N
    N
  

, affine structures do not always exist. It looks natural to consider affine structures as examples of polynomial structures of degree one. We introduce the concept of a canonical type polynomial structure for polycyclic-by-finite groups. Using the algebraic framework of the Seifert Fiber Space construction and a nice cohomology vanishing theorem, we prove the existence and uniqueness (up to conjugation) of canonical type polynomial structures for virtually finitely generated nilpotent groups. Applying this uniqueness to a result going back to Mal′cev, it follows that, for torsion-free, finitely generated nilpotent groups, each canonical polynomial structure is expressed in polynomials of limited degree. The minimal degree needed for obtaining a polynomial structure will determine the “affine defect number”. We prove that the known counterexamples to Milnor’s question have the smallest possible affine defect, i.e. affine defect number equal to one.
